---
title: "TSS vs Body Plots"
author: "Alos Diallo"
date: "2025-10-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Clear the R environment to start fresh
rm(list = ls())

# Load the library
library(AnnotationHub)

# 1. Connect to AnnotationHub
ah <- AnnotationHub()

# 2. Load the data using the correct ID that YOU found
correct_id <- "AH116484"
anno_hg38_gr <- ah[[correct_id]]

# 3. Convert the object to a standard data frame
anno_hg38 <- as.data.frame(anno_hg38_gr)

# 4. Define the CpGs you are looking for
cpgs_of_interest <- c("cg27498387", "cg09390241", "cg00377344")

# 5. Subset the data. The CpG IDs are the rownames.
my_cpg_info_hg38 <- anno_hg38[cpgs_of_interest, ]

# 6. Print the final results with the correct columns
# This data object should contain the standard Bioconductor column names.
print(my_cpg_info_hg38[, c("CHR", "MAPINFO", "UCSC_RefGene_Name", "UCSC_RefGene_Group")])
```


```{r}
# Load the annotation library
library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

# Get the full annotation data object from the package
anno_data <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

# Define the vector of CpG IDs you're interested in
cpgs_of_interest <- c("cg14556303")

# Subset the annotation data frame for your specific CpGs
my_cpg_info <- anno_data[cpgs_of_interest, ]

# Print the most relevant columns
print(my_cpg_info)
```
















```{r}
# Make sure the necessary libraries are loaded
library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# 1. Create a clean vector of your unique CpG IDs
blank_cpgs <- c(
    # Hyper-methylated
    "cg26750188", "cg06631010", "cg04888241", "cg10573232", "cg26166804",
    "cg16474170", "cg09304803", "cg10655471", "cg20964064", "cg07702509",
    "cg06224587", "cg24480555", "cg13831596", "cg14362370",
    # Hypo-methylated
    "cg16756764", "cg19221542", "cg10213101", "cg11895993", "cg24529549",
    "cg04165214", "cg24396358", "cg14501323", "cg27498387", "cg12691739",
    "cg18886245", "cg23355745", "cg21056723", "cg19020602", "cg26996201", "cg11287055"
)

# 2. Get the main annotation object using the correct function
full_annotation <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

# 3. Subset the annotation data for ONLY your blank CpGs
blank_cpg_details <- as.data.frame(full_annotation[blank_cpgs, ])

# 4. Display a clean table with just the key information
# This makes it easier to see the genes you were missing.
final_info <- blank_cpg_details %>%
  dplyr::select(UCSC_RefGene_Name, UCSC_RefGene_Group, Relation_to_Island) %>%
  rownames_to_column("CpG_ID")

print(final_info)

```

```{r}
# Make sure the rownames (CpG IDs) are a column we can work with
# install.packages("tibble") # If you don't have it
library(tibble)
cpg_details_df <- as.data.frame(blank_cpg_details) %>%
  rownames_to_column("CpG_ID")

# Create a new, clean data frame with just the info you need
final_table <- data.frame(
  CpG_ID = cpg_details_df$CpG_ID,
  
  # Get the first gene name before the semicolon ";"
  Gene = sapply(strsplit(cpg_details_df$UCSC_RefGene_Name, ";"), `[`, 1),
  
  # Get the first genomic location before the semicolon ";"
  Genomic_Location = sapply(strsplit(cpg_details_df$UCSC_RefGene_Group, ";"), `[`, 1)
)

# Print the clean, final table
print(final_table)
```


```{r}
###############################################################################
# gene_region_beta_plots_Mstats.R
# Purpose: For each gene of interest, show β-values (TSS vs Body) across
#          No Mets / LN-only / Distant, with paired Wilcoxon stats on M-values.
# Output : One PDF per gene + a combined CSV of stats.
###############################################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(readr); library(glue)
  library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)
  library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)
})

## ---- Inputs you can tweak ---------------------------------------------------
results_root <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Results"
out_dir      <- file.path(results_root, "GeneRegionSummaries")
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

beta_rds     <- file.path(results_root, "ewas_cache", "beta_comb_qc.rds")
meta_csv     <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/data_all.csv"

## Choose a small, hypothesis-driven set (edit this vector).
## Examples pulled from your significant contrasts:
# genes_of_interest <- c(
#   "PDCD1",       # (reference)
#   "SLIT2", "SOCS1", "COL11A1", "ZEB1", "INHBB",
#   "WNT7A", "WNT6", "ROBO1", "LCP1", "EPHB1", "ADAMTS18"
# )

genes_of_interest <- c(
  "JMJD8",       # (Top Hyper)
  "ADRA2C", "SLIT2", "ABL1", "CCDC60", "PTPRM",
  "PTPRM", "C8orf33", "KCNQ2", "ATP7B", "ATP7B", "SPTBN4","CYP24A1",
  "CAPN13",       # (Top Hypo)
  "CA10", "LINC00424", "ANK1", "GALNT14", "FER1L6-AS2",
  "PAK1", "C10orf11", "HLA-DOA", "LOC644145", "TFAP2B", "DSCR3", "APC","P53","KRAS"
)
## ---- Load β-matrix + meta ---------------------------------------------------
beta_comb <- {
  obj <- readRDS(beta_rds)
  if (is.list(obj) && !is.null(obj$beta)) obj$beta else obj
}
meta <- read_csv(meta_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    SampleID = paste(Sentrix_ID, Sentrix_Position, sep = "_"),
    mets_progression = case_when(
      Distant_Mets == 1 ~ "Distant Mets",
      ln_only == 1      ~ "LN-only Mets",
      TRUE              ~ "No Mets"
    ),
    mets_progression = factor(mets_progression,
                              levels = c("No Mets","LN-only Mets","Distant Mets"))
  ) %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, mets_progression)

common_ids <- intersect(colnames(beta_comb), meta$SampleID)
stopifnot(length(common_ids) > 0)
beta_comb <- beta_comb[, common_ids, drop = FALSE]
meta      <- filter(meta, SampleID %in% common_ids)

## ---- Annotation helper ------------------------------------------------------
anno <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19) %>% as.data.frame()

get_gene_cpgs <- function(gene_symbol, beta_mat, anno_df){
  df <- anno_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(UCSC_RefGene_Name == gene_symbol,
                  UCSC_RefGene_Group %in% c("TSS200","TSS1500","Body")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Name, UCSC_RefGene_Group) %>%
    dplyr::filter(Name %in% rownames(beta_mat))
  list(
    tss  = df %>% dplyr::filter(UCSC_RefGene_Group %in% c("TSS200","TSS1500")) %>% dplyr::pull(Name),
    body = df %>% dplyr::filter(UCSC_RefGene_Group == "Body") %>% dplyr::pull(Name)
  )
}

## ---- Transform + collapse ---------------------------------------------------
to_m <- function(b) log2((b + 1e-6) / (1 - b + 1e-6))  # safe logit2

collapse_region <- function(cpgs, beta_mat, label){
  if (!length(cpgs)) return(NULL)
  B <- beta_mat[cpgs, , drop = FALSE]
  M <- t(apply(B, 1, to_m))
  tibble(
    SampleID      = colnames(B),
    Region        = label,
    BetaCollapsed = colMeans(B, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mmean         = colMeans(M, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

## ---- Core function: one gene -> plot + stats row ----------------------------
analyze_gene <- function(gene_symbol, beta_mat, meta_df, anno_df, out_dir){
  cpgs <- get_gene_cpgs(gene_symbol, beta_mat, anno_df)
  if (!length(cpgs$tss) && !length(cpgs$body)) {
    message("Skipping ", gene_symbol, ": no TSS or Body CpGs present in matrix.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  df_tss  <- collapse_region(cpgs$tss,  beta_mat, "TSS")
  df_body <- collapse_region(cpgs$body, beta_mat, "Body")
  scores <- bind_rows(df_tss, df_body) %>%
    left_join(meta_df, by = "SampleID") %>%
    filter(!is.na(mets_progression))

  # If either region missing, still plot what exists; stats need both.
  have_both <- all(c("TSS","Body") %in% unique(scores$Region))
  stats_tbl <- NULL
  if (have_both) {
    df_wide <- scores %>%
      dplyr::select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, Mmean, mets_progression) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = c(BetaCollapsed, Mmean))

    stats_tbl <- df_wide %>%
      group_by(mets_progression) %>%
      group_modify(~{
        d <- .x
        ok <- complete.cases(d$Mmean_Body, d$Mmean_TSS)
        if (!any(ok)) return(tibble(
          n_pairs = 0, wilcox_p = NA_real_, HL_delta_M = NA_real_,
          HL_CI_low_M = NA_real_, HL_CI_high_M = NA_real_,
          median_delta_beta = NA_real_, mean_delta_beta = NA_real_
        ))
        wt <- wilcox.test(d$Mmean_Body[ok], d$Mmean_TSS[ok],
                          paired = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95,
                          alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE)
        delta_beta <- d$BetaCollapsed_Body[ok] - d$BetaCollapsed_TSS[ok]
        tibble(
          n_pairs            = sum(ok),
          wilcox_p           = wt$p.value,
          HL_delta_M         = unname(wt$estimate),
          HL_CI_low_M        = wt$conf.int[1],
          HL_CI_high_M       = wt$conf.int[2],
          median_delta_beta  = median(delta_beta, na.rm = TRUE),
          mean_delta_beta    = mean(delta_beta,  na.rm = TRUE)
        )
      }) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(gene = gene_symbol,
             wilcox_p_adj = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH")) %>%
      relocate(gene, .before = 1)
  }

  # Build plot labels (β-space)
  plot_df <- scores %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, mets_progression)
  lab_df <- NULL
  if (!is.null(stats_tbl)) {
    lab_df <- stats_tbl %>%
      mutate(label = glue("Wilcoxon (M): p(BH) = {formatC(wilcox_p_adj, format='e', digits=2)}\nΔβ median = {sprintf('%.3f', median_delta_beta)}")) %>%
      left_join(
        plot_df %>% group_by(mets_progression) %>% summarise(y = min(0.98, max(BetaCollapsed) + 0.06)),
        by = "mets_progression"
      )
  }

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Region, y = BetaCollapsed, fill = Region)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.75, color = "gray30") +
    geom_jitter(aes(group = SampleID), width = 0.15, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~ mets_progression) +
    { if (!is.null(lab_df)) geom_text(data = lab_df, aes(x = 1.5, y = y, label = label),
                                      size = 3.2, vjust = 0, inherit.aes = FALSE) } +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("TSS" = "#1f78b4", "Body" = "#e31a1c")) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = glue("{gene_symbol}: Body vs TSS methylation within metastasis groups"),
      x = NULL, y = "β-value (mean across CpGs per region)"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray50"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
    )

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, glue("{gene_symbol}_TSSvsBody_betaPlot.pdf")),
         p, width = 7.2, height = 5.0, device = cairo_pdf)

  stats_tbl
}
library(dplyr)
## ---- Run over all genes and save a combined stats table ---------------------
all_stats <- purrr::map_dfr(genes_of_interest, ~ analyze_gene(.x, beta_comb, meta, anno, out_dir))

if (nrow(all_stats)) {
  write_csv(all_stats, file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_population_stats.csv"))
}

cat("✓ Saved per-gene PDF plots to:\n  ", out_dir,
    "\n✓ Stats CSV (if any):\n  ", file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_population_stats.csv"), "\n")
```

```{r}
###############################################################################
# gene_region_beta_plots_Mstats.R (REVISED FOR ROBUST GENE FINDING)
# Purpose: For each gene of interest, show β-values (TSS vs Body) across
#          No Mets / LN-only / Distant, with paired Wilcoxon stats on M-values.
# Output : One PDF per gene + a combined CSV of stats.
###############################################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(readr); library(glue)
  library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)
  library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)
})

## ---- Inputs you can tweak ---------------------------------------------------
results_root <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Results"
out_dir      <- file.path(results_root, "GeneRegionSummaries")
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

beta_rds     <- file.path(results_root, "ewas_cache", "beta_comb_qc.rds")
meta_csv     <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/data_all.csv"

# Using official gene symbols for TP53, APC, and KRAS
genes_of_interest <- c("TCF7", 'BCL6', 'IL7R', 'CXCR5', 'SELL', 'LEF1','PDCD1', 'HAVCR2', 'LAG3', 'TIGIT', 'TOX', 'BATF', 'EOMES','IFNG', 'GZMB', 'PRF1', 'CXCL13' )

## ---- Load β-matrix + meta ---------------------------------------------------
beta_comb <- {
  obj <- readRDS(beta_rds)
  if (is.list(obj) && !is.null(obj$beta)) obj$beta else obj
}
meta <- read_csv(meta_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    SampleID = paste(Sentrix_ID, Sentrix_Position, sep = "_"),
    mets_progression = case_when(
      Distant_Mets == 1 ~ "Distant Mets",
      ln_only == 1      ~ "LN-only Mets",
      TRUE              ~ "No Mets"
    ),
    mets_progression = factor(mets_progression,
                              levels = c("No Mets","LN-only Mets","Distant Mets"))
  ) %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, mets_progression)

common_ids <- intersect(colnames(beta_comb), meta$SampleID)
stopifnot(length(common_ids) > 0)
beta_comb <- beta_comb[, common_ids, drop = FALSE]
meta      <- filter(meta, SampleID %in% common_ids)

## ---- Annotation helper ------------------------------------------------------
anno <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19) %>% as.data.frame()

# --- NEW, ROBUST GENE FINDING LOGIC ---
# This function correctly finds genes even with complex, semicolon-separated annotations.
get_gene_cpgs <- function(gene_symbol, beta_mat, anno_df){
  
  # Helper function to check for an EXACT match in a semicolon-separated list
  is_gene_match <- function(gene_string, target_gene) {
    target_gene %in% unlist(strsplit(as.character(gene_string), ";"))
  }
  
  # Apply this function to find all rows matching our gene of interest
  indices <- sapply(anno_df$UCSC_RefGene_Name, is_gene_match, target_gene = gene_symbol)
  
  df <- anno_df[indices, ] %>%
    dplyr::filter(UCSC_RefGene_Group %in% c("TSS200","TSS1500","Body")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Name, UCSC_RefGene_Group) %>%
    dplyr::filter(Name %in% rownames(beta_mat))
    
  list(
    tss  = df %>% dplyr::filter(grepl("TSS", UCSC_RefGene_Group)) %>% dplyr::pull(Name) %>% unique(),
    body = df %>% dplyr::filter(grepl("Body", UCSC_RefGene_Group)) %>% dplyr::pull(Name) %>% unique()
  )
}

## ---- Transform + collapse ---------------------------------------------------
to_m <- function(b) log2((b + 1e-6) / (1 - b + 1e-6)) # safe logit2

collapse_region <- function(cpgs, beta_mat, label){
  if (!length(cpgs)) return(NULL)
  B <- beta_mat[cpgs, , drop = FALSE]
  M <- t(apply(B, 1, to_m))
  tibble(
    SampleID      = colnames(B),
    Region        = label,
    BetaCollapsed = colMeans(B, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mmean         = colMeans(M, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

## ---- Core function: one gene -> plot + stats row ----------------------------
# This function remains unchanged as it works on the CpG lists provided by the new helper.
analyze_gene <- function(gene_symbol, beta_mat, meta_df, anno_df, out_dir){
  cpgs <- get_gene_cpgs(gene_symbol, beta_mat, anno_df)
  if (!length(cpgs$tss) && !length(cpgs$body)) {
    message("Skipping ", gene_symbol, ": no TSS or Body CpGs present in matrix.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  df_tss  <- collapse_region(cpgs$tss,  beta_mat, "TSS")
  df_body <- collapse_region(cpgs$body, beta_mat, "Body")
  scores <- bind_rows(df_tss, df_body) %>%
    left_join(meta_df, by = "SampleID") %>%
    filter(!is.na(mets_progression))

  have_both <- all(c("TSS","Body") %in% unique(scores$Region))
  stats_tbl <- NULL
  if (have_both) {
    df_wide <- scores %>%
      dplyr::select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, Mmean, mets_progression) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = c(BetaCollapsed, Mmean))

    stats_tbl <- df_wide %>%
      group_by(mets_progression) %>%
      group_modify(~{
        d <- .x
        ok <- complete.cases(d$Mmean_Body, d$Mmean_TSS)
        if (!any(ok)) return(tibble(n_pairs = 0, wilcox_p = NA_real_))
        
        wt <- wilcox.test(d$Mmean_Body[ok], d$Mmean_TSS[ok],
                          paired = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95,
                          alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE)
        delta_beta <- d$BetaCollapsed_Body[ok] - d$BetaCollapsed_TSS[ok]
        tibble(
          n_pairs           = sum(ok),
          wilcox_p          = wt$p.value,
          HL_delta_M        = unname(wt$estimate),
          HL_CI_low_M       = wt$conf.int[1],
          HL_CI_high_M      = wt$conf.int[2],
          median_delta_beta = median(delta_beta, na.rm = TRUE),
          mean_delta_beta   = mean(delta_beta,  na.rm = TRUE)
        )
      }) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(gene = gene_symbol,
             wilcox_p_adj = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH")) %>%
      relocate(gene, .before = 1)
  }

  plot_df <- scores %>% dplyr::select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, mets_progression)
  lab_df <- NULL
  if (!is.null(stats_tbl)) {
    lab_df <- stats_tbl %>%
      mutate(label = glue("Wilcoxon (M): p(BH) = {formatC(wilcox_p_adj, format='e', digits=2)}\nΔβ median = {sprintf('%.3f', median_delta_beta)}")) %>%
      left_join(
        plot_df %>% group_by(mets_progression) %>% summarise(y = min(0.98, max(BetaCollapsed, na.rm=TRUE) + 0.06)),
        by = "mets_progression"
      )
  }

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Region, y = BetaCollapsed, fill = Region)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.75, color = "gray30") +
    geom_jitter(aes(group = SampleID), width = 0.15, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~ mets_progression) +
    { if (!is.null(lab_df)) geom_text(data = lab_df, aes(x = 1.5, y = y, label = label),
                                      size = 3.2, vjust = 0, inherit.aes = FALSE) } +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("TSS" = "#1f78b4", "Body" = "#e31a1c")) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = glue("{gene_symbol}: Body vs TSS methylation within metastasis groups"),
      x = NULL, y = "β-value (mean across CpGs per region)"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray50"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
    )

  ggsave(file.path(out_dir, glue("{gene_symbol}_TSSvsBody_betaPlot.pdf")),
         p, width = 7.2, height = 5.0)

  stats_tbl
}

## ---- Run over all genes and save a combined stats table ---------------------
all_stats <- purrr::map_dfr(genes_of_interest, ~ analyze_gene(.x, beta_comb, meta, anno, out_dir))

if (nrow(all_stats) > 0) {
  write_csv(all_stats, file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_population_stats.csv"))
}

cat("✓ Saved per-gene PDF plots to:\n  ", out_dir,
    "\n✓ Stats CSV (if any):\n  ", file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_population_stats.csv"), "\n")

## ---- Facet plot: all genes together ----------------------------------------
if (nrow(all_stats) > 0) {

  # Recreate the long-format plotting data for all genes
  plot_data_all <- purrr::map_dfr(genes_of_interest, function(gene_symbol) {
    cpgs <- get_gene_cpgs(gene_symbol, beta_comb, anno)
    if (!length(cpgs$tss) && !length(cpgs$body)) return(NULL)
    df_tss  <- collapse_region(cpgs$tss,  beta_comb, "TSS")
    df_body <- collapse_region(cpgs$body, beta_comb, "Body")
    bind_rows(df_tss, df_body) %>%
      left_join(meta, by = "SampleID") %>%
      mutate(Gene = gene_symbol)
  }) %>%
    filter(!is.na(mets_progression))
  
  # Facet by gene and metastasis group
  p_all <- ggplot(plot_data_all, aes(x = Region, y = BetaCollapsed, fill = Region)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray30") +
    geom_jitter(aes(group = SampleID), width = 0.15, size = 0.6, alpha = 0.5) +
    facet_grid(mets_progression ~ Gene, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("TSS" = "#1f78b4", "Body" = "#e31a1c")) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = "All Genes: Body vs TSS β-values across metastasis groups",
      x = NULL,
      y = "β-value (mean across CpGs per region)"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray50"),
      strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
      strip.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "lines")
    )

  ggsave(
    file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_FacetPlot.pdf"),
    p_all, width = 14, height = 8
  )

  cat("✓ Saved combined facet plot:\n  ",
      file.path(out_dir, "AllGenes_TSSvsBody_FacetPlot.pdf"), "\n")
}

  # --- Set custom biological order for genes ---
  gene_order <- c(
    # Progenitor exhaustion
    "TCF7", "BCL6", "IL7R", "CXCR5", "SELL", "LEF1",
    # Intermediate exhaustion
    "PDCD1", "HAVCR2", "LAG3", "TIGIT", "TOX", "BATF", "EOMES",
    # Terminal exhaustion
    "IFNG", "GZMB", "PRF1", "CXCL13"
  )

  plot_data_all <- plot_data_all %>%
    mutate(Gene = factor(Gene, levels = gene_order))
```

```{r}
# Load the annotation library
library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

# Get the full annotation data object from the package
anno_data <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

# This first step correctly finds all CpGs associated with the TP53/WRAP53 locus
Gene_name_cpgs <- anno_data[grepl("KRAS", anno_data$UCSC_RefGene_Name), ]

# FIX: Use grepl() with an "OR" operator (|) to search for promoter terms
# This will find any entry that CONTAINS "TSS1500" or "TSS200"
Gene_promoter_cpgs <- Gene_name_cpgs[grepl("TSS1500|TSS200", Gene_name_cpgs$UCSC_RefGene_Group), ]

# Print the final, correctly filtered results
print(Gene_promoter_cpgs[, c("Name", "UCSC_RefGene_Name", "UCSC_RefGene_Group", "Relation_to_Island")])
```

Looking at TSS and Body methylation levels for PD-1 for Possible exhaustion story
```{r}
###############################################################################
# PDCD1_region_comparison_population.R
# Population-level test: Is Body methylation ≠ TSS within each mets group?
# - Plot β-values (TSS vs Body) with optional paired lines
# - Stats on M-values (paired Wilcoxon), with HL median difference + 95% CI
###############################################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(readr); library(glue)
  library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)
  library(ggplot2); library(ggpubr)
})

## ---- Inputs ----------------------------------------------------------------
results_root <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Results"
out_dir      <- file.path(results_root, "PDCD1_summary")
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

beta_rds     <- file.path(results_root, "ewas_cache", "beta_comb_qc.rds")
meta_csv     <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/data_all.csv"

## ---- Load beta matrix + meta ------------------------------------------------
beta_comb <- {
  obj <- readRDS(beta_rds)
  if (is.list(obj) && !is.null(obj$beta)) obj$beta else obj
}
meta <- read_csv(meta_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    SampleID = paste(Sentrix_ID, Sentrix_Position, sep = "_"),
    mets_progression = case_when(
      Distant_Mets == 1 ~ "Distant Mets",
      ln_only == 1      ~ "LN-only Mets",
      TRUE              ~ "No Mets"
    ),
    mets_progression = factor(mets_progression,
                              levels = c("No Mets","LN-only Mets","Distant Mets"))
  ) %>% select(SampleID, mets_progression)

common_ids <- intersect(colnames(beta_comb), meta$SampleID)
stopifnot(length(common_ids) > 0)
beta_comb <- beta_comb[, common_ids, drop = FALSE]
meta      <- filter(meta, SampleID %in% common_ids)

## ---- Annotation: PDCD1 CpGs (TSS + Body) -----------------------------------
anno <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19) %>% as.data.frame()
pdcd1_tbl <- anno %>%
  filter(UCSC_RefGene_Name == "PDCD1",
         UCSC_RefGene_Group %in% c("TSS1500","TSS200","Body")) %>%
  select(Name, UCSC_RefGene_Group) %>%
  filter(Name %in% rownames(beta_comb))

tss_cpgs  <- pdcd1_tbl %>% filter(UCSC_RefGene_Group %in% c("TSS1500","TSS200")) %>% pull(Name)
body_cpgs <- pdcd1_tbl %>% filter(UCSC_RefGene_Group == "Body") %>% pull(Name)
if (!length(tss_cpgs) && !length(body_cpgs)) stop("No PDCD1 TSS or Body CpGs present in matrix.")

## ---- Collapse per region (β for plot; M for stats) -------------------------
to_m <- function(b) log2((b + 1e-6) / (1 - b + 1e-6))  # safe logit2

collapse_region <- function(cpgs, beta_mat, region_label){
  B <- beta_mat[cpgs, , drop = FALSE]                    # CpG x sample (β)
  M <- apply(B, 1, to_m) %>% t()                         # CpG x sample (M)
  tibble(
    SampleID      = colnames(B),
    Region        = region_label,
    BetaCollapsed = colMeans(B, na.rm = TRUE),           # mean β across CpGs
    Mmean         = colMeans(M, na.rm = TRUE)            # mean M across CpGs
  )
}
df_tss  <- collapse_region(tss_cpgs,  beta_comb, "TSS")
df_body <- collapse_region(body_cpgs, beta_comb, "Body")

scores <- bind_rows(df_tss, df_body) %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(mets_progression))

## ---- Compute paired stats (Body vs TSS) per mets group ---------------------
# For each group, form paired vectors of Mmean_Body and Mmean_TSS per patient.
paired_stats <- scores %>%
  select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, Mmean, mets_progression) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = c(BetaCollapsed, Mmean)) %>%
  group_by(mets_progression) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    df <- .x
    # Paired Wilcoxon on M-values, with HL median difference & 95% CI
    wt <- wilcox.test(df$Mmean_Body, df$Mmean_TSS,
                      paired = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95,
                      alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE)

    # Also compute Δβ for interpretability
    delta_beta <- df$BetaCollapsed_Body - df$BetaCollapsed_TSS
    tibble(
      n_pairs         = sum(complete.cases(df$Mmean_Body, df$Mmean_TSS)),
      wilcox_p        = wt$p.value,
      HL_delta_M      = unname(wt$estimate),             # Hodges–Lehmann (Body−TSS) on M
      HL_CI_low_M     = wt$conf.int[1],
      HL_CI_high_M    = wt$conf.int[2],
      median_delta_beta = median(delta_beta, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_delta_beta   = mean(delta_beta,  na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  }) %>% ungroup() %>%
  mutate(wilcox_p_adj = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH"))

# Save stats
stats_csv <- file.path(out_dir, "PDCD1_TSSvsBody_population_stats.csv")
write_csv(paired_stats, stats_csv)

## ---- Build plot data (β-values) --------------------------------------------
plot_df <- scores %>%
  select(SampleID, Region, BetaCollapsed, mets_progression)

# Make label table to show BH-adjusted p per facet
lab_df <- paired_stats %>%
  mutate(label = glue::glue("Wilcoxon (M): p(BH) = {formatC(wilcox_p_adj, format='e', digits=2)}\nΔβ median = {sprintf('%.3f', median_delta_beta)}")) %>%
  left_join(
    plot_df %>% group_by(mets_progression) %>% summarise(y = min(0.98, max(BetaCollapsed) + 0.06)),
    by = "mets_progression"
  )

## ---- Plot: β-values with TSS vs Body side-by-side per group ----------------
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Region, y = BetaCollapsed, fill = Region)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.75, color = "gray30") +
  geom_jitter(aes(group = SampleID), width = 0.15, size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
  # optional: paired lines help reviewers see the within-patient direction
  # geom_line(aes(group = SampleID), color = "gray70", alpha = 0.5,
  #           position = position_dodge2(width = 0.6, preserve = "single")) +
  facet_wrap(~ mets_progression) +
  geom_text(data = lab_df, aes(x = 1.5, y = y, label = label),
            size = 3.4, vjust = 0, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TSS" = "#1f78b4", "Body" = "#e31a1c")) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "PDCD1: Body vs TSS methylation within metastasis groups",
    x = NULL, y = "β-value (mean across CpGs per region)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray50"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "PDCD1_TSSvsBody_population_plot.pdf"),
       p, width = 7.2, height = 5.0, device = cairo_pdf)

cat("✓ Saved:\n  -", stats_csv, "\n  -", file.path(out_dir, "PDCD1_TSSvsBody_population_plot.pdf"), "\n")
```
